heading: Stay of suit
label: 8892 Stay of suit
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)7722(a)(1st sentence, less applicability to duration of stay).46 U.S.C. 791(1st sentence, less applicability to duration of stay), .July 3, 1944, ch. 399, § 158 Stat. 7237722(b)(less 1st and 2d sentences and less proviso).46 U.S.C. 791(less 1st and 2d sentences and less proviso), .July 3, 1944, ch. 399, § 158 Stat. 723In subsection (a) the word “forthwith” is omitted as surplusage.In subsection (b) the words “of proceedings in pending suits as provided” are omitted as surplusage. The words “does not suspend” are substituted for the words “shall not operate to suspend”. The words “of the issues” and “the authority of” are omitted as surplusage. The words “issued before the stay” are substituted for the words “already issued at the time of such stay of suit”.

children.label: a
children.text: Whenever in time of war the Secretary of the Navy certifies to a court, or to a judge of a court, in which a suit described in is pending, that the prosecution of the suit would tend to endanger the security of naval operations in the war, or would tend to interfere with those operations, all further proceedings in the suit shall be stayed.section 8891 of this title

children.label: b
children.text: A stay under this section does not suspend the issue of process to take or preserve evidence to be used in the trial or prevent the completion of action under similar process issued before the stay.
